info.name: Pocket Inspector API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Pocket Inspector backend.

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection
2. Import `Pocket-Inspector.postman_environment.json` alongside it
3. Select the **Pocket Inspector — Local** environment
4. Run **Auth → Login as Admin** — tokens are saved automatically
5. All other requests will use the saved token

## Auto-token Flow
The **Login as Admin** and **Login as Inspector** requests include a test script that automatically saves `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `adminId`, and `inspectorId` to the active environment.

All requests use `{{accessToken}}` in the Authorization header via the collection-level auth setting.

request.description: Authenticates as admin. Automatically saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.
Authenticates as inspector. Saves tokens + inspectorId to collection variables.
Exchange a refresh token for a new token pair. New tokens are saved automatically.
Returns the authenticated user's profile. `passwordHash` is never included.
Returns the organisation the authenticated user belongs to.
Update the organisation name. Admin role required.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Returns all users in the authenticated admin's organisation.

exec: // Generate unique email to avoid 409 conflicts

request.description: Create a new user in the organisation. Roles: `ADMIN` | `INSPECTOR`.
Admin can get any user. Inspector can only get themselves.
Update user profile. Admin can update any user; inspector can only update themselves.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Returns all sites in the organisation (paginated).
Creates a site and automatically saves `siteId` to collection variables.
Permanently deletes a site. Returns 204 No Content on success.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Returns all buildings in the org. Filter by `siteId` is optional.
Creates a building and saves `buildingId` to collection variables.
Returns all floors for a specific building.
Returns a signed GCS URL. Upload the PDF directly to that URL, then call `Register Building Certificate`.
Call this after uploading to GCS to record the certificate. Notifies assigned inspectors.
Returns a short-lived signed URL to download the building's certificate PDF.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Creates a floor and saves `floorId` to collection variables.
Returns all doors for the floor with summary: status, imagesCount, certificatePresent.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Creates a door (status = DRAFT) and saves `doorId` to collection variables.
Returns full door detail including imagesCount and certificatePresent.

Returns a signed GCS URL (`uploadUrl`) and `objectPath`. Upload the image directly to `uploadUrl` using a PUT request with `Content-Type: image/jpeg`. Then call `Register Door Image`.

Roles allowed: `INSPECTOR` (and `ADMIN`).

Image roles: `FRONT` | `BACK` | `SIDE` | `LABEL` | `DAMAGE` | `OTHER`.
Call this after successfully uploading to GCS. Records the image in the database.

exec: // If GCS signed-upload failed (no imageId set), use placeholder values
// so Register Image can still run and allow door submission

request.description: Sets door status to SUBMITTED. Fails with 400 if no images have been uploaded. Use Inspector token.
Door must be in SUBMITTED status. Returns signed GCS URL for PDF upload.
Records the certificate. Sets door status to CERTIFIED. Notifies assigned inspectors via FCM.

exec: // Ensure admin token is used for door folder
// Create a fresh floor for door tests (since the Floors folder deleted floorId)
if (!buildingId) return; // Skip if no building created
// Only create if first request in this folder (avoid re-creating per request)
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ buildingId, label: 'D', notes: 'Floor for door tests' }) }

request.description: Admin sees all inspections in the org. Inspector only sees inspections they are assigned to.

Creates an inspection and saves `inspectionId`.

Types: `BUILDING` (requires `buildingId`) | `SITE` (requires `siteId`).
Assigns an inspector to an inspection. Inspector receives FCM push notification. Returns 400 if inspector is already assigned.
Inspector accepts or declines the assignment. Statuses: `ACCEPTED` | `DECLINED`. Returns 403 if already responded.

exec: // Switch to inspector token for this request

request.description: Moves inspection to ARCHIVED status.

exec: // Use admin token for inspection folder by default
pm.test('Export job created — exportJobId saved', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description:
Queues an async export job. The worker builds a ZIP of all images + certificates for the target.

Target types: `BUILDING` | `FLOOR` | `DOOR` | `SITE` | `INSPECTION`.

Poll `GET /v1/exports/:id` until status is `DONE`, then call `Get Export Download URL`.
Statuses: `QUEUED` → `RUNNING` → `DONE` | `FAILED`.
Returns a short-lived signed GCS URL to download the ZIP. Returns 400 if job is not DONE yet.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description:
Registers the mobile device's FCM token so the user can receive push notifications (assignment alerts, certificate issued, etc.).

Platforms: `IOS` | `ANDROID`.

Upserts the token — safe to call on every app launch.

exec: // Automatically use inspector token for this folder

request.description: Public endpoint. Returns app status and database connectivity + latency. No auth required.
